---
title: Queries & Mutations
description: The two building blocks of a quokkajs API — and how to use them.
---

## Queries vs. Mutations

quokkajs separates data operations into two types:

| | Query | Mutation |
|---|---|---|
| **Purpose** | Read data | Write data |
| **HTTP method** | `GET` (default) | `POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, `DELETE`, `HEAD` |
| **Caching** | Cached by default | Not cached |
| **Auto-refetch** | Yes, when invalidated | Triggers invalidation on success |
| **Hook suffix** | `Query` | `Mutation` |

---

## Auto-generated hook names

Hook names are derived from your endpoint key at runtime. The key is capitalised and the appropriate suffix is appended:

| Endpoint key | Builder type | Generated hook |
|---|---|---|
| `getTodos` | `builder.query` | `useGetTodosQuery` |
| `createTodo` | `builder.mutation` | `useCreateTodoMutation` |
| `searchImages` | `builder.query` | `useSearchImagesQuery` |
| `deletePost` | `builder.mutation` | `useDeletePostMutation` |
| `login` | `builder.mutation` | `useLoginMutation` |

---

## Using a query hook

Query hooks accept two arguments: the args to pass to your endpoint's `paramsFn`, and an options object.

```tsx
const { data, loading, initLoading, error, trigger } = useGetTodosQuery(args, options);
```

### Return value

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `data` | `Returns \| undefined` | Response data. `undefined` until a successful fetch. |
| `loading` | `boolean` | `true` while a request is in flight. |
| `initLoading` | `boolean` | `true` only during the first automatic fetch — useful for skeleton states. |
| `error` | `Error \| undefined` | Populated when the request fails or returns a non-2xx response. |
| `trigger` | `(args: Takes) => Promise<Returns \| undefined>` | Manually fire the request. |

### Query options

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="fetchOnRender" icon="play">
    **Type:** `boolean` — **Default:** `false`

    Automatically fire the query when the component first mounts.

    ```tsx
    const { data } = useGetTodosQuery('', { fetchOnRender: true });
    ```
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="fetchOnArgsChange" icon="rotate">
    **Type:** `boolean` — **Default:** `false`

    Re-fire the query whenever the `args` value changes between renders.

    ```tsx
    const [search, setSearch] = React.useState('');
    const { data } = useGetTodosQuery(search, { fetchOnArgsChange: true });
    // Re-fetches every time `search` changes
    ```
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="debouncedDuration" icon="clock">
    **Type:** `number` (milliseconds) — **Default:** `0`

    Debounce automatic fetches (triggered by `fetchOnRender` or `fetchOnArgsChange`) by the given number of milliseconds. Ideal for search-as-you-type.

    ```tsx
    const { data: images } = useSearchImagesQuery(
      { query, color },
      { fetchOnArgsChange: true, debouncedDuration: 500 }
    );
    // Won't fire until the user stops typing for 500ms
    ```
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="refetchOnFocus" icon="eye">
    **Type:** `boolean` — **Default:** `false`

    Re-fire the query when the browser window regains focus. Useful for keeping data fresh when users switch tabs.

    ```tsx
    const { data } = useGetTodosQuery('', {
      fetchOnRender: true,
      refetchOnFocus: true,
    });
    ```
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="pollingInterval" icon="arrows-rotate">
    **Type:** `number` (milliseconds)

    Re-fire the query on a recurring interval. The interval is cleared automatically when the component unmounts.

    ```tsx
    const { data } = useGetTodosQuery('', {
      fetchOnRender: true,
      pollingInterval: 30000, // re-fetch every 30 seconds
    });
    ```
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

---

## Using a mutation hook

Mutation hooks don't take args upfront — you pass them when you call `trigger`.

```tsx
const { data, loading, error, trigger } = useCreateTodoMutation();
```

### Return value

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `data` | `Returns \| undefined` | Response data from the last successful call. |
| `loading` | `boolean` | `true` while the mutation is in flight. |
| `error` | `Error \| undefined` | Populated when the request fails. |
| `trigger` | `(args: Takes) => Promise<Returns \| undefined>` | Fire the mutation. |

### Handling errors

`trigger` throws on failure. Use `try/catch` for imperative error handling:

```tsx
const { trigger, loading } = useCreateTodoMutation();

async function handleSubmit() {
  try {
    const result = await trigger({ title: 'Buy milk' });
    console.log('Created:', result);
  } catch (err) {
    // `error` state is also set automatically
    console.error('Failed:', err);
  }
}
```

---

## Examples

### Debounced image search

```tsx
import { useSearchImagesQuery } from './api/pexelsApi';

function ImageSearch() {
  const [query, setQuery] = React.useState('');

  const { data: images, loading } = useSearchImagesQuery(
    { query },
    { fetchOnArgsChange: true, debouncedDuration: 500 }
  );

  return (
    <div>
      <input
        value={query}
        onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)}
        placeholder="Search images..."
      />
      {loading && <p>Searching...</p>}
      <div>
        {images?.photos.map((img) => (
          <img key={img.id} src={img.src.medium} alt={img.alt} />
        ))}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

### Polling with focus refetch

```tsx
const { data: todos, loading } = useGetTodosQuery('', {
  fetchOnRender: true,
  refetchOnFocus: true,
  pollingInterval: 30000,
});
```

This pattern keeps a list fresh both on a 30-second timer and whenever the user returns to the tab.

### Optimistic form submission

```tsx
function LoginForm() {
  const [email, setEmail] = React.useState('');
  const { trigger, loading, error } = useLoginMutation();

  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    try {
      await trigger({ email });
    } catch {
      // error state is set automatically
    }
  };

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
      <input
        type="email"
        value={email}
        onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
      />
      <button type="submit" disabled={loading}>
        {loading ? 'Logging in...' : 'Log in'}
      </button>
      {error && <p style={{ color: 'red' }}>{error.message}</p>}
    </form>
  );
}
```
